Title: GDSL lipases modulate immunity through lipid homeostasis in rice

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize Ubiquitin1 (Ubi1), constitutive, for overexpression and GFP fusion; native OsGLIP1 promoter used for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycerolipid metabolism / galactolipid homeostasis linked to plant immunity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsGLIP1 is a negative regulator of resistance; knockout or down-regulation would be a plausible strategy to enhance broad-spectrum resistance to Xoo and M. oryzae. Because OsGLIP1 overexpression elevates MGDG/DGDG and compromises immunity, it should not be overexpressed for disease-resistance breeding. Deployment should consider possible perturbation of lipid homeostasis and photosynthesis-related galactolipids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a GDSL lipase that hydrolyzes lipid esters in vitro; in planta evidence suggests OsGLIP1 likely hydrolyzes TAG to influence DAG supply and thereby promote MGDG/DGDG accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression compromises rice resistance to bacterial blight and blast, while simultaneous RNAi silencing with OsGLIP2 enhances resistance; OsGLIP1 overexpression is associated with increased MGDG/DGDG and decreased TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathogen-responsive expression analysis; overexpression and RNAi transgenics; recombinant protein lipase assay with p-nitrophenyl acetate/butyrate; lipidomics by RP-HPLC/ESI/MS/MS; GFP localization; signal-peptide deletion analysis; pathogen infection assays; PR gene qRT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies or redirects precursor flux toward galactolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; salicylic acid treatment; BTH treatment; exogenous MGDG or DGDG feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and leaf sheaths; also detected in young lemma/panicle tissues. Expression is suppressed by Xoo infection, SA, and BTH.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and leaf sheaths; also detected in young lemma/panicle tissues. Expression is suppressed by Xoo infection, SA, and BTH.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route / remodeling step in lipid homeostasis upstream of galactolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome transcriptome analysis (previously performed, accession not provided); lipidomics by RP-HPLC/ESI/MS/MS and LC-MS/MS/HRMS (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo substrate specificity was not definitively proven. The discussion proposes OsGLIP1 preferentially affects TAG hydrolysis based on lipid-profile changes. Proper ER/lipid-droplet localization via the N-terminal signal peptide is necessary for immune-suppressing function.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAG/PA-to-DAG-associated branch feeding MGDG and DGDG homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering OsGLIP1 perturbs multiple lipid classes (TAG, DAG, MGDG, DGDG) and may affect growth-related lipid homeostasis; explicit yield penalty was not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely DAG-related precursor flux leading to increased MGDG and DGDG; exact direct product in planta not definitively established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Broad lipid esters in vitro; likely TAG in planta
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; conserved GDSL/V motif and catalytic residues Ser-Gly-Asn-His; N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; functions as an important control point in immunity-associated lipid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Lipid droplets; endoplasmic reticulum membrane / endomembrane vesicles; minor apoplastic signal possible
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGLIP1 is a functional GDSL lipase whose transcript is repressed by pathogen infection and SA/BTH treatment. 2. OsGLIP1 overexpression increases susceptibility to Xoo and M. oryzae, whereas RNAi co-silencing with OsGLIP2 enhances resistance and PR-gene induction. 3. OsGLIP1 alters leaf lipid composition, especially decreasing TAG and increasing MGDG/DGDG, linking lipid remodeling to suppression of rice immunity. 4. Its signal peptide-dependent localization to ER/lipid droplets is essential for immune inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane lipids important for photosynthetic membranes and plant development; in this study, elevated MGDG/DGDG attenuate rice disease resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize Ubiquitin1 (Ubi1), constitutive, for overexpression and GFP fusion; native OsGLIP2 promoter used for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycerolipid metabolism / galactolipid homeostasis linked to plant immunity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsGLIP2 is also a negative regulator of disease resistance; loss-of-function or down-regulation is a potential strategy for improving resistance. Avoid overexpression in resistance breeding because it elevates MGDG/DGDG and reduces immunity. Effects should be evaluated together with OsGLIP1 because the two genes act partially redundantly and synergistically.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a GDSL lipase that hydrolyzes lipid esters in vitro; in planta evidence suggests OsGLIP2 likely hydrolyzes PA, affecting DAG supply and thereby promoting MGDG/DGDG accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression compromises resistance to Xoo and M. oryzae, whereas simultaneous RNAi silencing with OsGLIP1 enhances resistance; OsGLIP2 overexpression is associated with reduced PA and increased MGDG/DGDG.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathogen-responsive expression analysis; overexpression and RNAi transgenics; recombinant protein lipase assay with p-nitrophenyl acetate/butyrate; lipidomics by RP-HPLC/ESI/MS/MS; GFP localization; pathogen infection assays; PR gene qRT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies or redirects precursor flux toward galactolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; salicylic acid treatment; BTH treatment; exogenous MGDG or DGDG feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in nodes, internodes and leaves; strong expression in elongating internodes. Expression is suppressed by Xoo infection, SA, and BTH.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in nodes, internodes and leaves; strong expression in elongating internodes. Expression is suppressed by Xoo infection, SA, and BTH.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route / remodeling step in lipid homeostasis upstream of galactolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome transcriptome analysis (previously performed, accession not provided); lipidomics by RP-HPLC/ESI/MS/MS and LC-MS/MS/HRMS (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo substrate specificity was not definitively proven. The discussion proposes OsGLIP2 preferentially affects PA hydrolysis based on lipid-profile changes. OsGLIP2 shows differential but complementary tissue expression relative to OsGLIP1.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PA-to-DAG-associated branch feeding MGDG and DGDG homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering OsGLIP2 perturbs PA, TAG and galactolipid homeostasis; explicit yield penalty was not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely DAG-related precursor flux leading to increased MGDG and DGDG; exact direct product in planta not definitively established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Broad lipid esters in vitro; likely phosphatidic acid (PA) in planta
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; conserved GDSL/V motif and catalytic residues Ser-Gly-Asn-His; N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; functions as an important control point in immunity-associated lipid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Lipid droplets; endoplasmic reticulum membrane / endomembrane vesicles; minor apoplastic signal possible
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGLIP2 is a functional GDSL lipase whose transcript is repressed by pathogen infection and SA/BTH treatment. 2. OsGLIP2 overexpression increases susceptibility to Xoo and M. oryzae, whereas RNAi co-silencing with OsGLIP1 enhances resistance and PR-gene induction. 3. OsGLIP2 alters leaf lipid composition, especially decreasing PA and increasing MGDG/DGDG, linking lipid remodeling to suppression of rice immunity. 4. OsGLIP2 and OsGLIP1 likely function synergistically with complementary expression patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane lipids important for photosynthetic membranes and plant development; in this study, elevated MGDG/DGDG attenuate rice disease resistance.
